summary: Implement a new 'Products' page (`ProductsPage.tsx`) with dynamically generated random product data. The page will display products using shadcn/ui Card components and include basic search/filter functionality.

steps.description: Create the `ProductsPage.tsx` file in the `client/src/pages/` directory. This file will contain the React component for the products page, responsible for generating and displaying random product data.
steps.details: Define a `Product` interface, create a function to generate an array of mock product data with random names, prices, and descriptions. Render these products in a grid layout using shadcn/ui `Card` components. Include a search input to filter products by name and a 'Add to Cart' button for each product. Use `useState` to manage the product list and search term.

steps.description: Integrate the new `ProductsPage` into the application's routing. This involves modifying `App.tsx` to add a new route that points to the `ProductsPage` component.
steps.details: Import `ProductsPage` and add a new `<Route>` component within the `<Routes>` block in `App.tsx`. The path for this route will be `/products`.

steps.description: Verify the new 'Products' page is accessible and displays correctly.
steps.details: Start the development server and navigate to `/products`. Confirm that the page loads, displays a list of products with random data, and that the search functionality works as expected. Check console for any errors.

risks: Potential for `react-router-dom` not being installed or configured, leading to routing errors.
Styling conflicts if global CSS or other UI libraries are present and not compatible with shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS.
Performance issues if a very large number of products are generated and rendered without virtualization (though unlikely for 20 products).
rollback_strategy: Revert changes to `client/src/App.tsx` by removing the new route. Delete the `client/src/pages/ProductsPage.tsx` file. This will remove the new page and its routing, returning the application to its previous state.
success_criteria: The application successfully compiles and runs. Navigating to `/products` displays a page titled 'Our Products' with a grid of randomly generated product cards. Each card shows an image, name, description, category badge, price, and an 'Add to Cart' button. The search input filters the displayed products dynamically.
original_request: create a products page with more random data.
